instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate the Legend Area
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Scan the drawing for a box, panel, or area labelled 'LEGEND', 'KEY', 'NOTATION', 'SYMBOL KEY', or 'DRAWING LEGEND'. This is typically found in the bottom-right or bottom-left corner of the drawing, within or adjacent to the title block. On multi-page drawings, the legend may appear only on page 1 — if so, note it as page 1. If no dedicated legend box is found, check for an unlabelled key or symbol table embedded in the drawing.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Describe Each Symbol Individually — Each Row Has Its Own Unique Symbol
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: CRITICAL: Every row in the legend has its own distinct drawn symbol. Each symbol_description must be unique — if you find yourself writing the same description for two or more consecutive rows, you have misread the legend and must look again. For LINE symbols: describe the line pattern precisely — solid/dashed/dotted/chain-dashed, thin/thick/heavy, and any decorations spaced along it (tick marks, letters like W or GAS, diagonal slashes, arrows). For POINT/ICON symbols: describe the outer shape (circle/square/rectangle/triangle), fill (hollow/solid black/half-filled), and any interior marks (cross, X, diagonal line, triangle, bowtie). Use the REFERENCE TABLE in the details field when you recognise a known symbol — match it and use the reference description. Ask yourself before writing each entry: 'Is this description unique and geometric? Could a reader draw exactly this symbol from my words alone without reading the label?'

instructional_sub_steps.title: Copy the Label Verbatim
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: For the 'label' field, copy the exact text written next to the symbol on the drawing, preserving capitalisation and spacing. Do not paraphrase or abbreviate. If a symbol has no label, record label as '[UNLABELLED]'. If a label has no visible symbol, record symbol_description as '[NO SYMBOL]'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Assign Category — MANDATORY for Every Entry
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: MANDATORY: 'category' is a required field for EVERY entry — NEVER null, NEVER omitted. If genuinely uncertain, use 'other'. PATTERN RULES — apply these in order: (1) 'cable': label contains any of TRENCH, CABLE, CONDUCTOR, OVERHEAD MAINS, UNDERGROUND MAINS, OVERHEAD LINE, DUCT, SPARE DUCT — examples: NEW LV TRENCH, EXISTING OVERHEAD MAINS, NEW UNDERGROUND MAINS, EXISTING UNDERGROUND CABLE, NEW OVERHEAD CONDUCTOR, REMOVE CONDUCTOR, REMOVE EXISTING OVERHEAD, STRING NEW OH CABLE. (2) 'equipment': label contains any of POLE, PILLAR, COLUMN, LANTERN, STREETLIGHT, SLCP, SL FLOOD, SL LANTERN, SL NIGHT WATCH, SHACKLE, JOINT, UGOH, HV ABS, HV USL, LV LINK, SWITCH, FUSE, METER, or label is a short equipment code (UGOH, IE 7.0 IA, IEB5, XXXXX treated as placeholder equipment code) — examples: EXISTING POLE, NEW POLE, REMOVE POLE, REPLACE POLE, EXISTING COLUMN, NEW COLUMN, EXISTING PILLAR, NEW PILLAR, EXISTING LANTERN, NEW LANTERN, REMOVE LANTERN, NEW SL FLOOD, EXISTING SL LANTERN, EXISTING SL NIGHT WATCH, NEW SLCP ZELL, EXISTING SLCP ZELL, REMOVE SL LANTERN, HV ABS (N/C), HV USL (N/C), EXISTING HV ABS CLOSE - REMOVE, NEW HV ABS CLOSE, EXISTING HV USL CLOSED, LV LINK (N/O), JOINT, END SHACKLE, UGOH. (3) 'substation': label contains SUBSTATION or KIOSK. (4) 'boundary': label contains EASEMENT, DEMARCATION, BOUNDARY, or LGA. (5) 'earthing': label is EARTHING or contains EARTH CONDUCTOR or EARTH ELECTRODE. (6) 'annotation': label is a dimension, callout, or arrow descriptor. (7) 'other': anything not matching above — includes TYPE 04 DUCT ARRANGEMENT, FOOTPATH, RMS ROAD, COMPACTED BACKFILL, POLYMERIC CABLE COVER, ROAD LEVEL, DUCT SPACING NTS, XXXXX (if not treating as equipment code). Apply pattern 1 first, then 2, then 3, then 4, then 5, then fall back to 'other'. NEVER leave category null.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Tag to Page Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Tag each entry with the page number from the chunk metadata. If the same legend appears on multiple pages, record it once tagged to page 1. If different pages have different or updated legends, record separate entries per page.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Exhaustiveness Check — Do Not Stop Early
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: After completing your initial extraction pass, count the total number of entries extracted. Then re-scan the legend panel to verify you have not stopped at a section boundary. Many legends are divided into multiple sections (cables, equipment, boundaries, duct arrangements, miscellaneous) separated by horizontal rules or section headings — ensure every section has been processed. DUCT ARRANGEMENT SECTION: Some drawings include a separate duct arrangement cross-section diagram area at the bottom of the legend panel, often showing a schematic cross-section of underground ducts. This area contains its own rows with labels such as DUCT WITH EXISTING CABLE, DUCT WITH NEW CABLE, NEW TRENCH, REMOVE CABLE, REMOVE DUCT & CABLE, REMOVE DUCT, SPARE DUCT, EARTHING, DUCT SPACING NTS — these are regular legend entries and MUST be extracted like any other row. Assign them category 'cable' for duct/cable/trench entries, 'earthing' for EARTHING, and 'other' for annotations like DUCT SPACING NTS. If you see a cross-section diagram in the legend area, always extract every labelled row from it. If the legend contains 10 or more visible rows but you have extracted fewer than 5 entries, you have missed most of the legend and must re-scan. Also verify: (1) every entry has the 'category' field present and is never null; (2) no entry has extra fields beyond the four required (page, symbol_description, label, category) — any other field name is a violation; (3) the 'label' field contains the exact verbatim text from the drawing, not a paraphrase. If any entry fails these checks, correct it before returning the result.

[STEP 7] Action: Extract Asset Spreadsheet Data
   [Step 7] Extract Asset Spreadsheet Data: Routing to Document Extractor (Port 8090)...
   Filtered to 1/3 files for 'TAL' (skipped 2).
   Extracted 1 file(s).
   Raw text fetched for 1 source PDF(s) for validation.
   Running independent validation...
   [WARN] Validation: The extraction process failed to include the 'superior_functional_location' field in asset records, which is a mandatory requirement. (86%)
   Files validated: input=None, output=AssetExtract_20260523_100235_285292.json

process_step.details: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=6 (document_category="TAL"). TAL files do not require chunking — run this step directly after Step 2 without needing Steps 3, 4, or 5. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those entries with document_category of exactly "TAL" (Technical Asset List). These are the spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) identified in Step 1. For each TAL document, use the proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet process as the extraction guide. Each spreadsheet entry from Step 2 includes worksheet_count (total sheets), worksheets_to_analyse (non-empty sheets), and a worksheets array listing each sheet name with its row_count and column_count — use these to plan how many extraction passes are needed. For each non-empty worksheet: identify the header row, map column names to standard asset fields (Asset ID, Serial Number, Asset Type, Description, Location, Address, Feeder, Voltage Level, Capacity Rating, Manufacturer, Model, Condition Rating, Status, Installation Date, Last Inspection Date), and extract every data row as a structured asset record. Include source_sheet and source_document in every record. Produce a consolidated output with all asset_records across all sheets and all TAL files. OUTPUT FILE: The agent saves a dedicated AssetExtract_{timestamp}.json file to the job output directory. This file contains: process_id, timestamp, source_documents (list of spreadsheet filenames), total_assets (integer), and asset_records (flat array of all extracted asset records across all sheets and all TAL files). This file is stored in state as asset_extraction and is available for use by Step 7 (report consolidation) and any subsequent steps.

expected_output.description: Step 1: Identify and Review Asset Spreadsheet Files — A DocumentReview JSON file listing all matched spreadsheet files with metadata and quality indicators. | Step 2: Plan Document Processing Approach — A ProcessingPlan JSON file with a processing entry per matched spreadsheet file. | Step 3: Extract Asset Records from Spreadsheet — Structured asset records extracted from the spreadsheet, with each record containing all available identification, classification, location, technical, and condition fields. | Step 4: POLE Worksheet Audit and Validation — A validation report for Pole assets highlighting missing fields, ID mismatches, and hierarchy errors.

fields.documents: Array of matched document objects, each with filename, document_title, document_type, author, date, confidence, content_type, text_quality, and recommended_reader
fields.scan_results: Full scan results for all files including those not matched
fields.total_files_scanned: Integer count of all files scanned
fields.output_file: Path to the saved ProcessingPlan_*.json file
fields.processing_plan: Array of processing entries, each with: filename, document_type, content_type, text_quality, recommended_reader, processing_tool_id, conversion_required, conversion_type, extraction_method, processing_notes
fields.total_documents: Integer count of entries in processing_plan
fields.asset_records: Array of asset record objects — each with: asset_id, serial_number, asset_type, description, location, address, feeder, voltage_level, capacity_rating, manufacturer, model, condition_rating, status, installation_date, last_inspection_date, superior_functional_location, source_sheet, source_row
fields.total_assets: Integer count of extracted asset records
fields.sheets_processed: Array of sheet names that were read from the spreadsheet
fields.source_documents: Array of spreadsheet filenames that contributed to the extracted asset records
fields.validation_errors: List of missing mandatory fields or hierarchy mismatches

sub_steps.details: Extract the unique identifiers for each asset record, including Asset ID, Asset Number, Serial Number, and any network operator assigned reference codes.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Asset ID Columns
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Identify the column(s) in the spreadsheet header row that serve as unique identifiers for each asset. Look for column names such as 'Asset ID', 'Asset Number', 'Asset Ref', 'ID', 'Tag Number', or 'Equipment Number'. Extract the value from each row.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Serial Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the 'Serial Number', 'Serial No', or 'S/N' column. Extract the manufacturer's serial number for each asset record. If no serial number column exists, record as null.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Record Network Operator References
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Identify any network operator specific reference codes (e.g., SAP asset number, MAXIMO work order, or network operator asset tag). These are typically in columns labelled 'SAP ID', 'Network Ref', or 'Operator Ref'.

sub_steps.details: Extract the asset type, category, and classification from each record. This includes the primary asset class (e.g., Transformer, Switchgear, Cable, Pole) and any sub-classification or asset group.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Asset Type Column
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the column(s) labelled 'Asset Type', 'Asset Category', 'Equipment Type', 'Asset Class', or 'Description'. Extract the asset type value for each row.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Asset Description
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Find the 'Description', 'Asset Description', or 'Equipment Description' column. Extract the full descriptive text for each asset record including make, model, and rated specification if combined in the description field.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Note Asset Group or Hierarchy
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If the spreadsheet contains parent-child asset relationships or asset group columns (e.g., 'Parent Asset', 'Asset Group', 'Functional Location'), extract these to preserve the asset hierarchy context.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Superior Functional Location (SFL)
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the column labelled 'Superior Function', 'Superior FL', 'Superior Functional Location', 'Functional Location', or 'FLOC'. This column contains the parent asset reference (e.g. 'POLE01011307' for a streetlight mounted on that pole, or 'CLMN00234567' for a lantern on that column). Extract the raw value exactly as written for each asset record and store it as 'superior_functional_location'. If no such column exists, record as null.

sub_steps.details: Extract all location and site information for each asset record, including physical address, substation reference, feeder reference, or geographic coordinates.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Address and Site
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate columns labelled 'Location', 'Address', 'Site', 'Site Name', or 'Suburb'. Extract the physical location for each asset including street address, suburb, or site identifier.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Network Location References
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Find columns for 'Feeder', 'Feeder ID', 'Substation', 'Zone Substation', 'Pole Reference', 'Span Reference', or 'Circuit'. These define where the asset sits within the electrical network topology.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Record GPS or Grid Coordinates
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If geographic coordinate columns exist (e.g., 'Latitude', 'Longitude', 'Easting', 'Northing', 'MGA', 'GDA'), extract the coordinate values for each asset.

sub_steps.details: Extract the technical rating and specification data for each asset including capacity, voltage level, manufacturer, model, and any rated parameters.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Capacity and Ratings
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate columns for 'Capacity', 'Rating', 'kVA', 'MVA', 'Amps', 'Current Rating', or 'Rated Capacity'. Extract the rated capacity with units for each asset. For transformers, capture both primary and secondary voltage ratings if available.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Voltage Level
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Find the 'Voltage Level', 'Voltage', 'kV', 'HV Voltage', or 'LV Voltage' column. Extract the operating voltage level for each asset expressed in kV or V.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Manufacturer and Model
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate 'Manufacturer', 'Make', 'Brand', 'Model', 'Model Number', or 'Type Number' columns. Extract the manufacturer name and model designation for each asset.

sub_steps.details: Extract the current condition rating, asset status (active, retired, proposed), and any maintenance or inspection flags for each asset record.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Condition Rating
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the 'Condition', 'Condition Rating', 'Condition Score', or 'Asset Health' column. Extract the condition rating for each asset. Condition is often expressed as a numeric score (1–5), a text grade (Good, Fair, Poor), or a percentage health index.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Asset Status
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Find the 'Status', 'Asset Status', 'Operational Status', or 'Active/Retired' column. Extract the current operational status for each asset (e.g., Active, In-Service, Retired, Proposed, Decommissioned).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Inspection and Maintenance Dates
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate date columns such as 'Installation Date', 'Commissioned Date', 'Last Inspection', 'Next Inspection', 'Last Maintenance', or 'Replacement Due'. Extract these dates for each asset record.

sub_steps.details: Verify the presence of the POLE tab and confirm it contains data before proceeding.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Worksheet Presence & Data Scan
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the tab labeled 'POLE'. If the tab is missing or contains no data records, mark as 'No Data' and terminate the POLE validation process. If data is present, proceed.

sub_steps.details: Extract and clean identifiers for cross-referencing with Design Drawings.

instructional_sub_steps.title: FLOC Cleaning Rule
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Extract Functional Location Numbers. Remove all leading letters and zeros for comparison (e.g., convert 'POLE00165863' to '165863').

instructional_sub_steps.title: Substation Superior Function Validation
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: For entries identified as 'Pole Substations', verify the 'Superior Function' field matches the mask 'POLEXXXXXXXXX'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Schedule Consistency Check
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Extract asset numbers from the 'POLE SCHEDULE TABLE' and verify consistency against the TAL and design drawings.

sub_steps.details: Check for mandatory data entry and hierarchical integrity between assembly records.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Mandatory Field Audit
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Ensure the following columns are populated: Special Use Code, EQ Reference Equipment Desc, EQ Technical Object Type, FLOC Technical Object, Location, and Equipment Owner.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Assembly & Hierarchy Validation
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: For every Assembly Number, verify: 1. Superior Functional Location matches the top-tab Functional Location. 2. FLOC Construction Type matches the design environment (Urban, Rural, Semi-Rural, Coastal). 3. Presence of Equipment Number and Technical Object attributes.

7.step_name: Match Assets to Site Plan Drawing Symbols
7.step_description: Using the asset records from Step 7, the text annotations extracted from the site plan in Step 5 (sub-step-extract-all-notes), and the drawing legend from Step 6, locate each asset in the site plan drawing text and identify which legend symbol is associated with it. For each asset record: (1) Strip all leading alphabetic prefix characters and any leading zeros from the asset_id to produce a bare numeric ID (e.g. SLGT00302876 → 302876, CB00045 → 45, TX0001234 → 1234). (2) Search the Step 5 extracted site plan text for the bare numeric ID as a text token or label — asset IDs appear as numeric labels adjacent to their drawing symbols in the annotated text. (3) When found, examine the text context immediately surrounding the bare ID to infer which drawing symbol is associated with it, then match that context to the best-fitting legend entry from Step 6 (comparing symbol descriptions and labels). Set symbol_description and label from the matched legend entry and match_status to 'found'. (4) If the bare ID is not found in the extracted text, set symbol_description and label to null and match_status to 'not_found'. Output an updated_assets array containing every original asset record from Step 7 with four new fields added: bare_id, symbol_description, label, and match_status. Also output asset_symbol_matches (the raw per-asset match results) and match_summary totals.

test_cases.input_data: Expected counts: total=14, found_direct=6, found_via_sfl=0, found_on_drawing=6, not_found=8.
test_cases.expected_output: Match summary should reflect the correct counts.
test_cases.actual_output: Match summary: total=14, found_direct=6, found_via_sfl=0, found_on_drawing=6, not_found=8.
test_cases.execution_notes: Compared the counts in the 'match_summary' object with the expected values.
test_cases.reasoning: The match summary accurately reflects the counts of each category, confirming correctness.

test_cases.description: Ensure that assets with 'direct', 'sfl_lookup', or 'sfl_chunk_search' methods have 'high' match confidence.
test_cases.input_data: Assets with match_method 'direct' or 'sfl_chunk_search'.
test_cases.expected_output: Assets should have match_confidence 'high'.
test_cases.actual_output: All relevant assets have match_confidence 'high'.
test_cases.execution_notes: Checked each asset's match_method and verified the match_confidence value.
test_cases.reasoning: All assets with specified match methods have the correct match confidence, ensuring consistency.

test_cases.description: Verify that enriched fields such as 'symbol_description', 'legend_label', and 'legend_category' are correctly populated.
test_cases.input_data: Enriched fields for assets with match_status 'found'.
test_cases.expected_output: Fields should be populated with non-null values for found assets.
test_cases.actual_output: Enriched fields are correctly populated for found assets.
test_cases.execution_notes: Reviewed each asset with match_status 'found' to ensure enriched fields are populated.
test_cases.reasoning: Enriched fields are correctly populated for assets with a match, maintaining data integrity.

test_cases.description: Ensure assets with match_status 'not_found' are correctly handled with appropriate fields.
test_cases.input_data: Assets with match_status 'not_found'.
test_cases.expected_output: Assets should have null values for enriched fields and match_confidence 'none'.
test_cases.actual_output: Assets have null values for enriched fields and match_confidence 'none'.
test_cases.execution_notes: Checked assets with match_status 'not_found' to ensure fields are null and match_confidence is 'none'.
test_cases.reasoning: Assets not found are correctly handled, with appropriate field values and match confidence.

8.step_description: Using the asset records from Step 7 and the symbol matches from Step 8, produce a fully enriched asset register via two-phase SFL linkage. Phase A — SFL lookup: for each asset whose own bare ID was not found in Step 8 (match_status 'not_found'), read the asset's 'superior_functional_location' field from the Step 7 TAL data (e.g. 'POLE01011307'), strip its prefix and leading zeros to a bare number (e.g. '1011307'), and check whether that bare number is present in Step 8's found_bare_map. If the superior asset WAS found on the drawing, inherit its symbol_description and legend label and set match_confidence to 'high'. Example: SLGT00316949 has superior_functional_location='POLE01011307'; bare SFL number is '1011307'; Step 8 found 1011307 with symbol 'Small hollow open circle' and label 'NEW POLE'; SLGT00316949 is therefore enriched with that label and confidence 'high'. Phase B — SFL chunk search: for assets whose SFL bare number was not in Step 8's found_bare_map (i.e. the superior asset was not itself in the TAL), search the site plan drawing chunks visually for the SFL bare number, and when found identify the adjacent symbol. Assets with no SFL field and those whose SFL was not found anywhere receive match_confidence 'none'.

Substation Clearances and Standards.description: Relocate existing pole 283061, 283062 & 283063 to the new road alignment as per MDI0031 requirements.

EarthingTechnicalRequirements.description: Install new Air break switch earthing as per EDI0006 & drawing B348252

SpecificEarthingStandards.description: All OH line design as per MDI0031 & MCI0005

LV Works.description: Remove existing OH LV conductor from pole 283062 to pole 426121.
Install new OH LV conductor 7/4.50AAC from new pole 2 to pole 426121.

Redirect the existing LV OH conductor to new poles 1 & 2. If the existing LV conductor length is insufficient, terminate existing LV conductor 7/4.50AAV on new pole 1 & install new span of LV bare 7/4.50AAC from new poles 1 to 2.

Compliance Standards.description: All OH line design as per MDI0031 & MCI0005.

Primary HV Method of Supply.description: Relocate existing pole 283061, 283062 & 283063 to the new road alignment as per MDI0031 requirements.
Remove existing OH HV conductor from poles 283061 to 283063.
Remove existing OH HV conductor from pole 283062 to pole 426121.
Install new ABS on new pole 3. New Air break switch earthing as per EDI0006 & drawing B348252.
Install new OH HV conductor 19/3.25 AAC Neptune from new poles 1 to 3.
Install new OH HV conductor 7/4.50 AAC from new pole 2 to pole 426121.

Compliance Standards.description: All OH line design as per MDI0031 & MCI0005.

Remittance and Restriction Requirements.description: Email for remittance copies: remittances@endeavourenergy.com.au. Credit cards and cheques are not accepted.

SL_Works.description: Relocate existing pole 283061, 283062 & 283063 to the new road alignment as per MDI0031 requirements.
Remove existing OH SL conductor from pole 283062 to pole 426121 and from pole 283062 to pole 283063.
Remove existing SLCP 704 on pole 283062.
Existing OH SL conductor to be redirected from pole 426129 to new pole 1 & pole 2.
Install new SL conductor 25mm^2 2C from new pole 2 to pole 426121 and from new pole 2 to pole 3.
Remove all existing street lighting on poles 283061 & 283063.
Install new streetlighting on new poles 1 & 3 as per LDI0001 requirements.
Install new additional luminaires and light pole as per the lighting assessment.

proc-extract-design-brief-info.summary: Review and extract key engineering and funding information from a submitted design brief document package. The process identifies all submitted documents, reads each matched document, and extracts structured data covering supply requirements (HV, LV, SL, Earthing, Easements, and Substation) and determination of funding requirements, producing a structured JSON output per document and a consolidated process output.
proc-extract-design-brief-info.search_context: Design brief documents for a customer connections application submitted to Endeavour Energy. Target documents include: project design briefs (PDF documents containing a 'Method of Supply Requirement' section and a 'Determination of Funding Requirements' section), customer connection offer documents, and any associated architectural, services, or town planning reports. Key filename indicators: documents with 'DESIGN_BRIEF', 'BRIEF', or 'ARP' (Endeavour Energy project reference) in the filename. Key content indicators: structured sections covering HV Works, LV Works, SL Works, Earthing, Easements, Substation works, and a funding determination table listing items with responsible party and funding category. Exclude: reticulation drawings and engineering drawing files identified by 'RETIC' or 'DWG' in the filename — these are Site Plan documents and must be assigned document_category 'Site Plan'.

process_input.description: A folder containing the design brief document package for a project, including all relevant PDFs and supporting documents submitted by the applicant.
process_input.format: Folder path containing PDF, DOCX, or other supported document types

process_output.description: A structured JSON object containing the extracted supply requirements and funding determination items from the design brief, linked to the source document and section.

document_categorisation.description: Rules for identifying and categorising a document as a 'Design Brief' document. When Step 2 (Plan Document Processing Approach) assigns a 'document_category' field to each entry in the processing plan, it must apply these rules. A document must satisfy at least two positive indicators and must not satisfy any negative indicators to be categorised as 'Design Brief'.

filename_patterns: Contains 'DB' as a standalone component of the filename
Contains 'ARP' followed by a project number (typical Endeavour Energy design brief naming convention)
Contains 'DESIGN' in conjunction with project or ARP references
structural_indicators: Contains a structured table of supply requirements across HV, LV, SL, Earthing categories
Contains a funding determination section listing items with responsible party and funding category
Is a text-based PDF document (not a drawing or diagram)
Contains project metadata fields: project name, project number, applicant name, date

negative_indicators.description: If any of these are present, the document must NOT be categorised as 'Design Brief' — classify it as 'Site Plan' instead.
filename_patterns: Contains 'SUBSTATION' or 'substation' as the primary descriptor (not as a section heading within a brief)
content_keywords: HV Switchgear (as an extracted annotation field in a drawing table)
Transformer Size (as an annotation in a drawing callout)
Cubicle Size (as a drawing annotation field)

document_categorisation.decision_rule: If the document contains a 'Method of Supply' or 'Determination of Funding Requirements' section, classify as 'Design Brief'. If it contains a 'Substation Notes' annotation table or is a technical engineering drawing, classify as 'Site Plan'. When uncertain, prefer 'Design Brief' for text-based documents and 'Site Plan' for drawing/diagram files.

steps.step_name: Identify and Review Design Brief Documents
steps.summary: Scan the submitted document package folder, assess each file against the design brief search context, and produce a structured list of matched documents with extracted metadata. Identifies which documents are relevant and classifies each by type.
steps.details: Upload the design brief package folder path to the Document Reviewer Agent. The agent lists all files in the folder, assesses each filename for relevance using an LLM, reads file content for matched candidates, and performs a content match analysis. Returns a ranked list of matched documents with metadata including document title, type, author, date, project name, and project number. Only documents with overall_confidence > 0.5 are included in the matched documents list. The output file is saved to the OUTPUT directory.

expected_input.description: A folder path containing the design brief document package.

fields.folder_path: Absolute path to the folder containing documents (e.g. /documents/ARP4983-84/)
fields.search_context: Description of the document types being searched for (generated from task definition)

expected_output.description: A DocumentReview JSON file listing all matched documents with metadata and quality indicators.

fields.documents: Array of matched document objects, each with filename, document_title, document_type, author, date, project_name, project_number, revision, confidence, content_type, text_quality, and recommended_reader
fields.scan_results: Full scan results for all files including those not matched
fields.total_files_scanned: Integer count of all files scanned
fields.output_file: Path to the saved DocumentReview_*.json file

actual_input.source: User-provided folder path via the frontend folder prompt

steps.summary: Read the Step 1 document review output and the tool registry to determine the best processing tool for each matched document, whether format conversion is needed, and the extraction method to use.
steps.details: The Document Processing Planner reads the Step 1 DocumentReview output file and the registry.json. For each document in the matched list, it uses an LLM to assess the document's content_type and text_quality and selects the most appropriate processing tool from the registry. If a document is image-based or has no extractable text (text_quality = none), conversion to PNG for OCR is flagged as required. Outputs a ProcessingPlan JSON file with one entry per document.

expected_input.description: The Step 1 DocumentReview output file path, automatically retrieved from the orchestrator state.

fields.step1_output_file: Path to the DocumentReview_*.json file produced by Step 1

expected_output.description: A ProcessingPlan JSON file with a processing entry per matched document.

fields.processing_plan: Array of processing entries, each with: filename, document_type, content_type, text_quality, recommended_reader, processing_tool_id, conversion_required, conversion_type, extraction_method, processing_notes
fields.total_documents: Integer count of entries in processing_plan
fields.output_file: Path to the saved ProcessingPlan_*.json file

actual_input.source: Automatically passed from Step 1 output — no user input required

steps.step_name: Extract Supply Requirements from Design Brief
steps.summary: For each matched design brief document, extract all supply requirement information including HV, LV, SL, Earthing, Easements, and Substation requirements. Each item is linked to its source document and section.
steps.details: Using the processing plan from Step 2, each matched document is read using the tool specified in processing_tool_id (e.g. tool-extract-pdf-content). The extracted sections are passed to an LLM which identifies and extracts all supply-related requirements across six categories, each handled as a dedicated sub-step. Each extracted requirement records the source section, page number, and verbatim or summarised requirement text.

sub_steps.details: Identify and consolidate all High Voltage (HV) information by navigating technical requirements, visual markups, and funding determinations.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Primary HV Method of Supply (MOS)
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Review the 'Method of Supply Requirement' section. Identify: 1) Removal of Assets (e.g., OH HV cables between specific pole refs). 2) Installation of Assets (e.g., new OH HV cable locations). 3) Voltage Specifics (verify line types as HV or LV, checking for mislabeled requirements in LV sections).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Review Visual HV Markups and Annotations
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Examine the site sketch. Cross-reference text with visual data to identify: 1) Cable Specifications (e.g., 'NEW 240M XEE HV Cable' or 'New OH 7/4.5AMC'). 2) Connection Points (e.g., '2-UGOH Poles'). 3) Substation Context (e.g., 'New Sub' locations).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify HV Infrastructure Funding and Materials
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Determine financial responsibilities. Identify: 1) Reimbursements (e.g., Spare HV duct capital contributions). 2) Customer Charges (e.g., Duct usage charges). 3) Contestability (e.g., Network switching or substation commissioning).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Verify Compliance Standards
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Ensure HV design meets mandatory codes. Verify requirements for compliance with MDI0031, MCI0005, and NSW Service and Installation Rules.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Consolidate all High Voltage Information
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Group and format all information which relates to High Voltage actions, materials, standards, markups and methods of supply into a single category High Voltage (HV).

sub_steps.details: Based on the provided Design Brief (ARP4983), here are the instructions to locate all information specifically regarding Low Voltage (LV) works for the Elizabeth Street project:

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify the LV Works Section
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Navigate to the section of the document under the heading Method of Supply Requirement. Locate the sub-heading LV Works to find the specific scope of electrical adjustments.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Review Removal and Installation Requirements
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Find the instruction to remove the Overhead (OH) LV cable currently located between pole ref '23' and pole ref '20' on the southern side of Elizabeth Drive. Note the requirement to string a new OH cable between pole ref '3' and pole ref '20' on the northern side of Elizabeth Drive. Note: While the brief label for this specific bullet point mentions 'HV cable,' it is categorized strictly under the 'LV Works' section.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Consult the Visual Sketch
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Refer to the diagram to visualize the physical path of the LV asset relocation. Look for the legend or mark-ups indicating 'Remove OH' and 'New OH' to see how the LV lines interact with the northern and southern sides of the road.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Verify Compliance Standards
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Ensure all LV service works comply with the NSW Service and Installation Rules. Confirm the design adheres to Endeavour Energy standards MDI0031 and MCI0005.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Check Service Connections
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the General section regarding the 'New OH road crossing service connection' intended to maintain existing services.

sub_steps.details: Identify and extract all Street Lighting (SL) information by reviewing specialized work sections and visual indicators.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify the SL Works Section
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the 'SL Works' sub-heading under the Method of Supply Requirement section. Identify specific tasks such as 'Remove SL' and 'Install new SL'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Cross-reference with Visual Markups
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Examine the provided sketch for markers or annotations specifically relating to street lighting assets. Verify if SL locations are tied to specific pole references mentioned in HV or LV sections.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Check for SL Material Funding
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Review the funding determination section to see if Street Lighting materials are 'Endeavour Energy Supplied' or if they fall under 'Customer Funded Contestable Works'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Verify SL Standard Compliance
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Ensure extraction captures any requirement for SL works to comply with specific public lighting standards or council requirements mentioned in the text.

sub_steps.details: Extract all technical requirements regarding grounding and earthing systems for the proposed electrical assets.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Earthing Technical Requirements
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search the Design Brief for keywords 'Earthing', 'Grounding', or 'Earth Mat'. Identify requirements for earthing at new substation or switching station locations.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Specific Earthing Standards
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Extract references to mandatory earthing standards (e.g., MDI or MCI series) that must be met during the detailed design phase.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Check Substation Specific Earthing
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Review the 'Substation & Switching Station Works' section to determine if specific earthing configurations are required for the new pole substation or padmount substation.

sub_steps.details: Identify and document all land interest, easement, and property access requirements specified in the brief.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Land Interest Guidelines
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Identify the 'Land Interests' section in the brief. Verify if the checkbox for 'NA' is selected or if land interests are required for the project.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Required Easements
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Extract details regarding required easements for new HV/LV lines or substation sites. Note any reference to the 'Land Interest Guidelines (LIG)'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Check for Property Access Conditions
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Document any specific conditions regarding access to private property or the creation of right-of-ways for Endeavour Energy assets.

sub_steps.details: Capture all specifications and requirements for the removal, installation, or modification of substation assets.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Substation Works Section
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Identify the 'Substation & Switching Station Works' sub-heading. Record specific actions like 'Remove Pole Sub' or 'Install New Pole Sub' at specific references.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Technical Ratings and References
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Document any stated ratings (e.g., kVA) or specific equipment references (e.g., 'ref 9' or 'ref 29') for the substation assets.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Substation Commissioning Responsibilities
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Review the funding section to determine if 'substation commissioning' is categorized as a 'Customer Funded Non-Contestable Work'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Verify Substation Clearances and Standards
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Capture requirements for physical clearances or compliance with substation design standards like MDI0031 mentioned in the compliance section.

expected_input.description: The ProcessingPlan from Step 2 indicating which tool to use per document, and the document files themselves.

fields.processing_plan: Array of processing entries from Step 2 output
fields.documents_folder: Folder path containing the actual document files

expected_output.description: Structured supply requirements extracted from the design brief documents.

supply_requirements.hv: Array of High Voltage requirement objects — each with section, page, and requirement text
supply_requirements.lv: Array of Low Voltage requirement objects
supply_requirements.sl: Array of Street Lighting requirement objects

fields.source_documents: Array of document filenames that contributed to the extracted requirements

actual_input.source: Automatically passed from Step 2 processing_plan; documents read via tool-extract-pdf-content

steps.summary: For each matched design brief document, extract all items listed under the Determination of Funding Requirements section, capturing each funding line item with its description, amount (if stated), responsibility party, and source section.
steps.details: Using the section-level extracted content from Step 3 (or re-reading the document if needed), an LLM is prompted to identify and extract all content under the 'Determination of Funding Requirements' heading or equivalent section. The LLM extracts each listed item individually, recording: item description, funding category (e.g. capital contribution, non-contestable, contestable), responsible party (e.g. applicant, network operator), monetary amount or formula if stated, and the source section and page. The output is a structured funding requirements list linked to the source document.

sub_steps.details: Prompt the LLM to identify and extract all items listed under the 'Determination of Funding Requirements' section or equivalent heading. For each item record: item_description (verbatim or paraphrased), funding_category (e.g. capital contribution, non-contestable, contestable), responsible_party (e.g. applicant, network operator), amount_or_formula (monetary amount or formula if stated), source_section, and source_page.

Prompt the LLM to identify and extract all line items listed under 'Ancillary Network Services Fees', 'Payment Advice', or 'Pro Forma Tax Invoice' sections. For each fee record: item_description (e.g. Standard Connection Offer Fee, Design Information Fee), date, amount (dollar value), and source_section and source_page. Also capture the total amount payable if stated.
Identify and consolidate all logistics required for the applicant to remit payment to Endeavour Energy. [cite: 93]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Preferred Payment Method
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the preferred payment channel, specifically identifying EFT as the primary method. [cite: 94]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Banking Details
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Capture the specific financial coordinates provided, including the BSB (012-003) and Account Number (8376 89858). [cite: 94]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Mandatory Transaction References
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the unique alphanumeric reference code (e.g., ARP4983A2) that must be quoted in the EFT transaction field. [cite: 95]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Document Remittance and Restriction Requirements
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Identify the email for remittance copies (remittances@endeavourenergy.com.au) and note that credit cards and cheques are not accepted. [cite: 95, 96]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Capture Invoice Policy
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Note the condition for receiving a formal Tax Invoice following the receipt of payment. [cite: 97]

expected_input.description: The extracted section content from Step 3 (or the raw document content), along with the matched document list.

fields.extracted_sections: Section-level content from Step 3 tool output (text, tables per section)

expected_output.description: Structured funding determination items extracted from the design brief.

funding_requirements.items: Array of funding requirement objects — each with: item_description, funding_category, responsible_party, amount_or_formula, source_section, source_page, source_document
funding_requirements.total_items: Integer count of extracted funding items

actual_input.source: Extracted section content from tool-extract-pdf-content output or direct document re-read

consolidated_output.description: A single consolidated output JSON combining the results of all steps for the process run.

extraction_categories.name: Easement Restriction on Use of Land
keywords: RESTRICTION ON THE USE OF LAND

extraction_categories.name: Padmount Substation Easement
keywords: CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY

validation_directives.name: Complete notes extraction from every page
validation_directives.description: Verify that all numbered notes and standalone ATTENTION/WARNING/CAUTION/NOTICE callouts from every page are captured in the notes extraction. Notes are often split across quadrant chunks — confirm no note numbers are missing from the sequence.

validation_directives.name: Substation data extraction from Substation Notes
validation_directives.description: Verify that the Substation Notes annotation block has been located and all seven structured data fields have been extracted: asset number, transformer size, HV switchgear (with conditional rule applied), voltage level, LV switchgear, cubicle size, and earthing. Each field should be sourced from the Substation Notes table, not inferred.

validation_directives.name: Conditional rule: 1.5MVA transformer requires Siemens RLR
validation_directives.description: When transformer size is 1.5MVA, verify HV Switchgear is recorded as 'Siemens RLR' and the override note 'Overridden per 1.5MVA rule' is included in the substation data output.

validation_directives.name: Assets field-checked compliance note
validation_directives.description: Verify the note 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' is present on the drawing and answered 'YES'. If the note is present but answered 'NO', flag as non-compliant. If the note is absent entirely, flag as NOT FOUND.

validation_directives.name: Restriction on use of land (swimming pool setback) note
search_keywords: RESTRICTION ON THE USE OF LAND
validation_directives.description: Check whether the required restriction-on-use-of-land note is present: 'A RESTRICTION ON THE USE OF LAND, IN RELATION TO SWIMMING POOLS, MEASURED 5m FROM THE SUBSTATION EASEMENT IS TO BE CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT [xxx]'. Extract the lot number(s) referenced. If not present, record as NOT REQUIRED (only required for projects with substation easements).

validation_directives.name: Padmount substation easement note
search_keywords: CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY
validation_directives.description: Check whether the required padmount substation easement note is present: 'AN EASEMENT FOR PADMOUNT SUBSTATION MINIMUM [x]m x [x]m IS TO BE CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT [xxxx]'. Extract the easement dimensions and lot number(s). If not present, record as NOT REQUIRED (only required for projects involving padmount substations).

validation_directives.name: Drawing legend completeness
validation_directives.description: Verify that the drawing legend has been fully extracted from every page that contains one. Each legend entry must include a clear description of the visual symbol or line style and its associated text label. Entries must be tagged to the correct page number. Symbols without labels and labels without symbols both indicate an incomplete extraction.

conditional_rules.description: If transformer size is 1.5MVA, HV switchgear type must be recorded as Siemens RLR regardless of drawing annotation. This rule is applied within the Substation Data sub-step and must be noted explicitly in the output.
conditional_rules.override_note: Overridden per 1.5MVA rule — Siemens RLR required regardless of drawing annotation.

proc-extract-site-plan-info.summary: Review and extract key engineering data from a submitted site plan document package. The process runs in four steps: Steps 1–2 identify and plan document processing; Step 3 performs two-phase extraction of all drawing notes and annotations (Phase 1) and analyses those notes against four compliance checks — Substation Data, Assets Field Check, Easement Restriction, and Easement Substation (Phase 2); Step 4 extracts the drawing legend from every page using vision, producing a structured list of every symbol, icon, and line style alongside its associated label, tagged to the source page number so the legend can be used to identify assets shown on the drawing.
proc-extract-site-plan-info.search_context: Site plan drawings for an electrical network project submitted to Endeavour Energy. Target documents include: electrical network diagrams showing HV and LV infrastructure, substation layout drawings (pole substations, padmount substations, kiosk substations), site layout plans with electrical asset annotations, and engineering drawings containing a 'Substation Notes' or 'Sub Notes' callout table. Key filename indicators: documents with 'RETIC' (reticulation drawing), 'DWG' (engineering drawing), or a drawing number prefix (e.g. D followed by digits, A followed by digits) in the filename. Key content indicators: a Substation Notes annotation block, a NOTES section with numbered notes, ATTENTION/WARNING/CAUTION callouts, easement creation notes, and field check compliance notes. Engineering drawings produced to Endeavour Energy or similar network operator standards. Target file types: PDF engineering drawings, scanned drawing sets. Exclude: design brief documents identified by 'BRIEF' or 'DESIGN_BRIEF' in the filename.

process_input.description: A folder containing the site plan document package for a project, including all relevant PDFs and engineering drawings.
process_input.format: Folder path containing PDF or other supported document types

process_output.description: Structured extraction output containing all drawing notes, four compliance and data checks, and a structured drawing legend.

fields.drawing_notes: Complete numbered notes list and all ATTENTION/WARNING/CAUTION callouts from every page, in sequence order
fields.substation_data: Formatted string containing all Substation Notes fields: Asset Number, Transformer Size, HV Switchgear (with override notation if 1.5MVA rule applied), Voltage Level, LV Switchgear, Cubicle Size, Earthing
fields.field_check_compliance: 'YES', 'NO', or 'NOT FOUND' — result of checking for the assets field-checked note
fields.easement_restriction: Extracted easement restriction note text with lot number, or 'NOT REQUIRED' if not applicable
fields.easement_substation: Extracted padmount substation easement note text with dimensions and lot number, or 'NOT REQUIRED' if not applicable
fields.drawing_legend: Array of legend entry objects extracted from all pages — each entry has: page (integer), symbol_description (string — visual geometry of the drawn symbol such as shape, fill, line weight, line pattern, e.g. 'Small open circle with radiating lines' or 'Hollow rectangle outline' — NOT a paraphrase of the label), label (string — verbatim label text), category (string). Entries are ordered by page number then by position in the legend.

document_categorisation.description: Rules for identifying and categorising a document as a 'Site Plan' document.

filename_patterns: Contains 'RETIC' or 'retic' (e.g. ARP4983_RETIC, D526458_ARP4983_RETIC — indicates a reticulation drawing)
Contains 'DWG' as part of the filename (indicates an engineering drawing)
Contains 'SUBSTATION' or 'SUB' as the primary descriptor (not as a section heading within a brief)
Drawing number prefix format (e.g. D followed by digits, A followed by digits) typical of network operator drawing numbering conventions
Contains 'ELEC', 'ELECTRICAL', or 'NETWORK' in conjunction with a drawing reference
structural_indicators: Contains a 'Substation Notes' or 'Sub Notes' callout table or annotation block
Is a PDF produced from a CAD or drawing system (not a word-processed document)
Contains a drawing title block with drawing number, revision, and scale
Contains electrical network symbols: transformer symbols, cable route lines, substation symbols, pole symbols
Filename follows a drawing number convention (e.g. D526458, A534927)

negative_indicators.description: If any of these are present, the document must NOT be categorised as 'Site Plan' — classify it as 'Design Brief' instead.
filename_patterns: Contains 'ARP' as a standalone prefix without a drawing number suffix
content_keywords: Method of Supply Requirement (as a section heading — indicates a design brief)
Determination of Funding Requirements (as a section heading — indicates a design brief)
HV Works, LV Works, SL Works (as structured sub-headings within a supply requirements section)

document_categorisation.decision_rule: If the document contains a 'Substation Notes' annotation table or is an engineering drawing with a title block and drawing number, classify as 'Site Plan'. If it contains a 'Method of Supply' or 'Determination of Funding Requirements' section, classify as 'Design Brief'. When uncertain, prefer 'Site Plan' for drawing/diagram files and 'Design Brief' for text-based PDF documents.

steps.step_name: Identify and Review Site Plan Documents
steps.summary: Scan the submitted document package folder, assess each file against the site plan search context, and produce a structured list of matched documents with extracted metadata. Identifies which documents are electrical network drawings, substation drawings, or site layout plans, and classifies each by type.
steps.details: Upload the site plan package folder path to the Document Reviewer Agent. The agent lists all files in the folder, assesses each filename for relevance using an LLM (targeting electrical network diagrams, substation layout drawings, and site plans), reads file content for matched candidates, and performs a content match analysis. Returns a ranked list of matched documents with metadata including document title, type, author, date, drawing number, and revision. Only documents with overall_confidence > 0.5 are included in the matched documents list. The output file is saved to the OUTPUT directory.

expected_input.description: A folder path containing the site plan document package.

fields.folder_path: Absolute path to the folder containing site plan documents
fields.search_context: Site plan drawings for an electrical network project, including electrical network diagrams, substation layout drawings, and site layout plans with electrical asset annotations.

expected_output.description: A DocumentReview JSON file listing all matched site plan documents with metadata and quality indicators.

fields.documents: Array of matched document objects with filename, document_title, document_type, author, date, drawing_number, revision, confidence, content_type, text_quality, and recommended_reader
fields.total_files_scanned: Integer count of all files scanned
fields.output_file: Path to the saved DocumentReview_*.json file

actual_input.source: User-provided folder path via the frontend folder prompt

steps.summary: Read the Step 1 document review output and the tool registry to determine the best processing tool for each matched site plan document, whether format conversion is needed (e.g. scanned PDF to PNG for OCR), and the extraction method to use.
steps.details: The Document Processing Planner reads the Step 1 DocumentReview output file and the registry.json. For each document in the matched list, it uses an LLM to assess the document's content_type and text_quality and selects the most appropriate processing tool from the registry. Site plan drawings are frequently image-based (scanned or plotted to PDF without selectable text); if a document is image-based or has no extractable text (text_quality = none), conversion to PNG for OCR is flagged as required. Outputs a ProcessingPlan JSON file with one entry per document.

expected_input.description: The Step 1 DocumentReview output file path, automatically retrieved from the orchestrator state.

fields.step1_output_file: Path to the DocumentReview_*.json file produced by Step 1

expected_output.description: A ProcessingPlan JSON file with a processing entry per matched site plan document.

fields.processing_plan: Array of processing entries, each with: filename, document_type, content_type, text_quality, recommended_reader, processing_tool_id, conversion_required, conversion_type, extraction_method, processing_notes
fields.total_documents: Integer count of entries in processing_plan
fields.output_file: Path to the saved ProcessingPlan_*.json file

actual_input.source: Automatically passed from Step 1 output — no user input required

steps.summary: Two-phase extraction from each matched site plan drawing. Phase 1 extracts all notes and annotations from every page. Phase 2 analyses those notes against four checks: Substation Data, Assets Field Check, Easement Restriction, and Easement Substation.
steps.details: Using the processing plan from Step 2, each matched site plan document is processed using the tool specified in processing_tool_id (OCR via chunk-based vision for image-based drawings). The extraction runs in two logical phases within a single pass: Phase 1 captures every note, annotation, and callout from every page in full — numbered notes (1., 2., 3., ...), ATTENTION: callouts, WARNING: callouts, CAUTION: callouts, and NOTICE: callouts. Phase 2 analyses those captured notes to produce four structured outputs: (1) Substation Data extracted from the Substation Notes annotation block, (2) verification of the assets field-check compliance note, (3) check for the swimming pool restriction-on-use-of-land easement note, and (4) check for the padmount substation easement note.

sub_steps.sub_step_name: Extract All Drawing Notes and Annotations
sub_steps.details: Extract every textual note and annotation from every page of the drawing in full. This is a comprehensive raw extraction pass — do not filter or summarise. Capture: all numbered notes (1., 2., 3., ...) from the NOTES section with their complete text; all standalone ATTENTION:, WARNING:, CAUTION:, and NOTICE: callout blocks with their complete text; any inline hand-written or red-line markup annotations. Notes that span multiple drawing regions must be assembled into a single complete entry. This output is the source material for all Phase 2 sub-steps.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate and Extract Numbered Notes Block
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Scan every page of the drawing for a dedicated NOTES section or numbered annotation block. Extract ALL individual notes listed, in sequence order (1., 2., 3., ...). Each note must be captured in full — do not truncate. If a note is split across adjacent image regions (common in large-format drawings), assemble the complete text. Include every note number in the final output — missing numbers indicate an incomplete extraction.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Standalone Callout Annotations
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: In addition to the numbered notes block, scan every page for standalone callout boxes or annotations with labels ATTENTION:, WARNING:, CAUTION:, or NOTICE:. These may appear anywhere on the drawing (not just in the NOTES block) and often contain critical safety, service, or access requirements. Extract each callout's label and its complete text. If the callout appears physically separate from the NOTES block, include it as an additional entry appended after the numbered notes (e.g. 'CAUTION: HIGH PRESSURE GAS MAINS IN VICINITY...').

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Red-line and Hand-written Markups
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Look for hand-written or red-line markup annotations that modify or supplement the printed notes — for example, specific cable types, removal or addition instructions, or project-specific notes. Capture these verbatim and identify them as markups (e.g. '[MARKUP] Remove span between...').

sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, locate the Substation Notes annotation block and extract the seven structured data fields. Apply the conditional HV Switchgear rule: if Transformer Size is 1.5MVA, HV Switchgear must be recorded as 'Siemens RLR' regardless of what is written in the drawing, and the override must be explicitly noted in the output. Return all seven fields as a formatted multi-line string.
sub_steps.output_format:
Multi-line formatted string:
Substation Asset Number: [value or NOT FOUND]
Transformer Size: [value or NOT FOUND]
HV Switchgear: [value — if 1.5MVA rule applied, append: OVERRIDDEN per 1.5MVA rule — Siemens RLR required]
Voltage Level: [value or NOT FOUND]
LV Switchgear: [value or NOT FOUND]
Cubicle Size: [value or NOT FOUND]
Earthing: [value or NOT FOUND]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Substation Notes Block
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search the extracted notes and the drawing for annotations labelled 'Substation Notes', 'Sub Notes', or a similar heading. These are typically found in a table or callout box near the substation symbol or in the drawing title block area. Extract the heading and all fields within it.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract the Seven Data Fields
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: From the Substation Notes block, extract each of the following fields as labelled: Substation Asset Number (unique ID assigned by the network operator, e.g. S1234 or a 6-digit number), Transformer Size (rated capacity with units, e.g. 1.5MVA, 500kVA, 200kVA), HV Switchgear (make and model, e.g. Siemens RLR, ABB SafeRing, or as written), Voltage Level (in kV, e.g. 11kV, 22kV, 33kV), LV Switchgear (make, model, and rating), Cubicle Size (dimensions or type code), and Earthing (type and compliance standard).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Apply 1.5MVA Conditional HV Switchgear Rule
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Check the extracted Transformer Size. If it equals 1.5MVA (or equivalently 1500kVA), then regardless of what the drawing shows for HV Switchgear, record HV Switchgear as 'Siemens RLR' and append the note 'OVERRIDDEN per 1.5MVA rule — Siemens RLR required.' to that field. If the transformer size is not 1.5MVA, record HV Switchgear exactly as written in the Substation Notes.

sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, verify that the drawing includes the mandatory assets field-check compliance note. The exact note required is: 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' answered with 'YES'. Return one of three values: 'YES' (note present, answered YES), 'NO' (note present, answered NO — flag as non-compliant), or 'NOT FOUND' (note is absent from the drawing — flag for follow-up).
sub_steps.required_note_text: HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING

instructional_sub_steps.title: Search for Field Check Note
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search all extracted notes for the text 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' or a close paraphrase. This note is typically numbered within the NOTES section and followed by 'YES' or 'NO'. Note: in the drawing the word 'DRAWING' may appear as 'DESIGN' — treat both as equivalent.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Read and Return the Answer
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Once the note is located, read the answer that follows it ('YES' or 'NO'). Return 'YES' if the answer is YES. Return 'NO' if the answer is NO (and add a flag: 'NON-COMPLIANT — field check not confirmed'). If the note cannot be found anywhere in the drawing, return 'NOT FOUND' (and add a flag: 'MISSING — this compliance note is mandatory').

sub_steps.sub_step_name: Easement — Restriction on Use of Land (Swimming Pool Setback)
sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, check whether the drawing includes the required restriction-on-use-of-land note relating to swimming pools within the substation easement setback. This note is required for any project that creates a substation easement. Extract the full note text including the lot number(s) referenced. If the note is absent, determine whether this is a padmount substation project — if so, flag as MISSING; if not, flag as NOT REQUIRED.
sub_steps.required_note_template: A RESTRICTION ON THE USE OF LAND, IN RELATION TO SWIMMING POOLS, MEASURED 5m FROM THE SUBSTATION EASEMENT IS TO BE CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT [xxx]
sub_steps.output_format: Full extracted note text with lot number(s), or 'NOT REQUIRED' (not a substation easement project), or 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED' (substation easement project but note is missing)

instructional_sub_steps.title: Search for Restriction Note
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search all extracted notes for text containing 'RESTRICTION ON THE USE OF LAND', 'SWIMMING POOL', '5m FROM THE SUBSTATION EASEMENT', or 'IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT'. This note may appear within the numbered NOTES section or as a separate annotation.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Note Text and Lot Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If found, extract the full note text verbatim. Identify and record the lot number(s) referenced after 'WITHIN PROPOSED LOT' — this is a critical compliance detail. Return the complete extracted text as the field value.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Determine Applicability if Not Found
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If the note is not found, check whether the project involves a padmount substation (look for 'padmount', 'PADMOUNT', or 'PAD MOUNT' in the Substation Notes or drawing annotations). If a padmount substation is involved, this note is mandatory — return 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED (padmount substation project detected)'. If no padmount substation is present, return 'NOT REQUIRED'.

sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, check whether the drawing includes the required padmount substation easement creation note. This note is required for all projects involving a padmount substation. Extract the full note text including the easement dimensions and lot number(s) referenced. If the note is absent and the project involves a padmount substation, flag as MISSING.
sub_steps.required_note_template: AN EASEMENT FOR PADMOUNT SUBSTATION MINIMUM [x]m x [x]m IS TO BE CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT [xxxx]
sub_steps.output_format: Full extracted note text with easement dimensions and lot number(s), or 'NOT REQUIRED' (not a padmount substation project), or 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED' (padmount substation project but note is missing)

instructional_sub_steps.title: Search for Padmount Easement Note
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search all extracted notes for text containing 'EASEMENT FOR PADMOUNT SUBSTATION', 'PADMOUNT SUBSTATION MINIMUM', or 'CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT' alongside a dimension or measurement. This note may appear within the numbered NOTES section or as a standalone annotation.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Easement Dimensions and Lot Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If found, extract the full note text verbatim. Specifically identify: (a) the easement dimensions stated after 'MINIMUM' (e.g. '3m x 4m', '4.5m x 6m') and (b) the lot number(s) referenced after 'WITHIN PROPOSED LOT'. Both details are critical — record them clearly in the output.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Determine Applicability if Not Found
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If the note is not found, check whether the project involves a padmount substation (look for 'padmount', 'PADMOUNT', or 'PAD MOUNT' in the Substation Notes or drawing annotations). If a padmount substation is involved, this note is mandatory — return 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED (padmount substation project detected)'. If no padmount substation is present, return 'NOT REQUIRED'.

expected_input.description: The ProcessingPlan from Step 2 indicating which tool to use per site plan document, and the document files themselves.

fields.processing_plan: Array of processing entries from Step 2 output
fields.documents_folder: Folder path containing the actual site plan document files

expected_output.description: Five structured extraction outputs per document: complete drawing notes, substation structured data, field check compliance result, and two easement check results.

fields.sub-step-extract-all-notes: Complete list of all numbered notes and standalone callouts from every page, in sequence order
fields.sub-step-substation-data: Formatted string with all seven Substation Notes fields, with 1.5MVA override notation where applicable
fields.sub-step-field-check: 'YES', 'NO', or 'NOT FOUND' result for the assets field-check compliance note
fields.sub-step-easement-restriction: Extracted restriction-on-use-of-land note text with lot number, or 'NOT REQUIRED' / 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED'
fields.sub-step-easement-substation: Extracted padmount substation easement note text with dimensions and lot number, or 'NOT REQUIRED' / 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED'

actual_input.source: Automatically passed from Step 2 processing_plan; image-based documents processed via vision chunk pipeline

steps.summary: Extract the complete legend (key) from every page of each site plan drawing using vision. Produces a structured list of every symbol, icon, and line style paired with its associated label, tagged to the page it was extracted from. This output is used to identify assets shown on the drawing.
steps.details: For each image-based site plan document from Step 2, scan every page for a LEGEND, KEY, or NOTATION section. Legends on engineering drawings typically appear in the title block area, a dedicated key box, or a corner panel. Each entry in the legend consists of a visual element (an icon, symbol, filled shape, or line pattern) paired with a text label that names the asset or feature it represents. The extraction must capture both the visual description of the symbol and the exact text label, and tag each entry to the page number on which the legend appears. Symbols that appear in the legend but lack a label, and labels without a matching visual element, must both be recorded as incomplete entries for review.

sub_steps.sub_step_name: Extract Legend Entries from All Pages
sub_steps.details: Scan every image chunk of the drawing for a legend, key, notation, or symbol table. Process every visible row in the legend panel from top to bottom — do NOT stop at the end of one section before checking for additional sections. Many legends have 2–4 distinct sections (e.g. cables, then equipment, then boundaries, then duct arrangements) separated by dividers or headings; continue until you have reached the last row. For each row, extract: (1) the VISUAL GEOMETRY of the drawn symbol — describe its exact shape, fill, line weight, line pattern, and any interior marks as you see them drawn; (2) the exact text label; (3) the page number; (4) the category — REQUIRED for every entry, no exceptions. CRITICAL RULE: symbol_description must describe what the symbol LOOKS LIKE as a drawn graphic, NOT a paraphrase or echo of the label text. Each legend row has its own unique symbol — do not assign the same visual description to multiple rows. If you see the same description repeating for consecutive rows, you have misread the legend — look again at each row individually. WRONG examples (label paraphrases — never produce these): 'New lantern', 'Existing pole', 'New pillar', 'Remove conductor', 'Underground cable'. CORRECT examples (visual geometry): 'Small hollow circle with short lines radiating outward from the edge', 'Small solid black filled circle', 'Hollow rectangle outline with no fill', 'Solid thin line with evenly spaced short perpendicular tick marks'. REFERENCE TABLE — use these known symbol descriptions when you recognise the visual pattern next to the label: NEW LV TRENCH → Long dashed line; STRING NEW OH CABLE → Short dashed line; EXISTING UNDERGROUND MAINS → Alternating long-short dashed line; EXISTING OH CABLE → Solid thin line; REMOVE CONDUCTOR → Dotted line; EXISTING DUCTS → Thick heavy solid black line; NEW HV TRENCH → Solid line with two diagonal slash marks; EXISTING LANTERN → Small circle with internal cross (four-quadrant cross inside circle); REMOVE LANTERN → Circle with internal cross and starburst outer spikes radiating from edge; NEW LANTERN → Circle with starburst outer spikes and hollow centre; EXISTING POLE → Small solid black filled circle; REMOVE POLE → Small solid black filled circle with large X through it; REPLACE POLE → Circle split vertically half-black half-white; NEW POLE → Small hollow open circle; EXISTING COLUMN → Small solid black filled square; NEW COLUMN → Small hollow open square; EXISTING PILLAR → Solid black filled rectangle; NEW PILLAR → Hollow rectangle outline; PADMOUNT SUBSTATION → Rectangle containing two triangles touching at their points (bowtie shape); POLE SUBSTATION → Circle with triangle inside; LV LINK (N/O) → Small circle with short vertical ticks on top and bottom; HV ABS (N/C) → Circle with horizontal line through the centre; HV USL (N/C) → Semi-circle dome with horizontal base line; HIGH PRESSURE GAS → Solid line with GAS text centred along it; WATER MAIN → Solid line with W characters spaced evenly along it; LGA DEMARCATION → Thin solid line; NEW FREEWAY BOUNDARY → Faint light grey long dashed line; ENDEAVOUR ENERGY EASEMENT → Faint light grey medium dashed line. When you see a symbol that matches one of these, use the reference description exactly.
sub_steps.output_format:
JSON array of legend entry objects. STRICT SCHEMA: Each object must contain EXACTLY these four fields — no more, no fewer: 'page' (integer, 1-based), 'symbol_description' (string — visual geometry only), 'label' (string — verbatim label text), 'category' (string — REQUIRED, see values below). STRICT FIELD PROHIBITION: Any field name other than 'page', 'symbol_description', 'label', and 'category' is strictly prohibited — this includes but is not limited to: legend_text, label_text, bounding_box, label_position, notes, id, section, type, geometry, or ANY other key you might think of. If you find yourself wanting to add another field, do NOT — instead map the information into symbol_description or label. Extra fields cause downstream comparison failures and will invalidate the entire extraction. CATEGORY RULES — 'category' is MANDATORY for every entry without exception — NEVER null. Use these PATTERN RULES in priority order: (1) 'cable' — label contains TRENCH, CABLE, CONDUCTOR, OVERHEAD MAINS, UNDERGROUND MAINS, OVERHEAD LINE, DUCT, or SPARE DUCT (examples: NEW LV TRENCH, EXISTING OVERHEAD MAINS, NEW UNDERGROUND MAINS, EXISTING UNDERGROUND CABLE, EXISTING OH CABLE, NEW OVERHEAD CONDUCTOR, REMOVE CONDUCTOR, REMOVE EXISTING OVERHEAD, STRING NEW OH CABLE, SPARE DUCT, DUCT WITH EXISTING CABLE, REMOVE DUCT, REMOVE CABLE, REMOVE DUCT & CABLE, NEW TRENCH); (2) 'equipment' — label contains POLE, PILLAR, COLUMN, LANTERN, SLCP, SL FLOOD, SL LANTERN, SL NIGHT WATCH, SHACKLE, JOINT, UGOH, HV ABS, HV USL, LV LINK, SWITCH, or FUSE, OR is a short equipment code (examples: EXISTING POLE, NEW POLE, REMOVE POLE, REPLACE POLE, EXISTING COLUMN, NEW COLUMN, EXISTING PILLAR, NEW PILLAR, EXISTING LANTERN, NEW LANTERN, REMOVE LANTERN, NEW SL FLOOD, NEW SL LANTERN, EXISTING SL LANTERN, EXISTING SL NIGHT WATCH, NEW SLCP ZELL, EXISTING SLCP ZELL, REMOVE SL LANTERN, HV ABS (N/C), HV USL (N/C), EXISTING HV ABS CLOSE - REMOVE, NEW HV ABS CLOSE, EXISTING HV USL CLOSED, LV LINK (N/O), JOINT, END SHACKLE, UGOH, IE 7.0 IA, IEB5, TYPE 2 RAGBOLT); (3) 'substation' — label contains SUBSTATION or KIOSK; (4) 'boundary' — label contains EASEMENT, DEMARCATION, BOUNDARY, or LGA; (5) 'earthing' — label is EARTHING or contains EARTH; (6) 'annotation' — dimension or callout; (7) 'other' — anything not matched above (e.g. TYPE 04 DUCT ARRANGEMENT, FOOTPATH, RMS ROAD, COMPACTED BACKFILL, POLYMERIC CABLE COVER, ROAD LEVEL, DUCT SPACING NTS, XXXXX). If genuinely uncertain, ALWAYS use 'other' — NEVER leave category null or absent. Each row in the legend must produce a UNIQUE symbol_description — if two consecutive entries have identical symbol_descriptions you have made an error and must re-examine each row. Example output:
[
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Long dashed line", "label": "NEW LV TRENCH", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Solid thin line", "label": "EXISTING OH CABLE", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Dotted line", "label": "REMOVE CONDUCTOR", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Thick heavy solid black line", "label": "EXISTING DUCTS", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Small hollow open circle", "label": "NEW POLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Small solid black filled circle", "label": "EXISTING POLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Small solid black filled circle with large X through it", "label": "REMOVE POLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Asterisk", "label": "EXISTING HV ABS CLOSE - REMOVE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Filled circle", "label": "END SHACKLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Circle with starburst outer spikes and hollow centre", "label": "NEW LANTERN", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Hollow rectangle outline", "label": "NEW PILLAR", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Rectangle containing two triangles touching at their points", "label": "PADMOUNT SUBSTATION", "category": "substation" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Thin solid line", "label": "LGA DEMARCATION", "category": "boundary" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Faint light grey medium dashed line", "label": "ENDEAVOUR ENERGY EASEMENT", "category": "boundary" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Solid line with W characters spaced evenly along it", "label": "WATER MAIN", "category": "other" }
]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate the Legend Area
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Scan the drawing for a box, panel, or area labelled 'LEGEND', 'KEY', 'NOTATION', 'SYMBOL KEY', or 'DRAWING LEGEND'. This is typically found in the bottom-right or bottom-left corner of the drawing, within or adjacent to the title block. On multi-page drawings, the legend may appear only on page 1 — if so, note it as page 1. If no dedicated legend box is found, check for an unlabelled key or symbol table embedded in the drawing.
