instructional_sub_steps.title: Describe Each Symbol Individually — Each Row Has Its Own Unique Symbol
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: CRITICAL: Every row in the legend has its own distinct drawn symbol. Each symbol_description must be unique — if you find yourself writing the same description for two or more consecutive rows, you have misread the legend and must look again. For LINE symbols: describe the line pattern precisely — solid/dashed/dotted/chain-dashed, thin/thick/heavy, and any decorations spaced along it (tick marks, letters like W or GAS, diagonal slashes, arrows). For POINT/ICON symbols: describe the outer shape (circle/square/rectangle/triangle), fill (hollow/solid black/half-filled), and any interior marks (cross, X, diagonal line, triangle, bowtie). Use the REFERENCE TABLE in the details field when you recognise a known symbol — match it and use the reference description. Ask yourself before writing each entry: 'Is this description unique and geometric? Could a reader draw exactly this symbol from my words alone without reading the label?'

instructional_sub_steps.title: Copy the Label Verbatim
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: For the 'label' field, copy the exact text written next to the symbol on the drawing, preserving capitalisation and spacing. Do not paraphrase or abbreviate. If a symbol has no label, record label as '[UNLABELLED]'. If a label has no visible symbol, record symbol_description as '[NO SYMBOL]'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Assign Category — MANDATORY for Every Entry
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: MANDATORY: 'category' is a required field for EVERY entry — NEVER null, NEVER omitted. If genuinely uncertain, use 'other'. PATTERN RULES — apply these in order: (1) 'cable': label contains any of TRENCH, CABLE, CONDUCTOR, OVERHEAD MAINS, UNDERGROUND MAINS, OVERHEAD LINE, DUCT, SPARE DUCT — examples: NEW LV TRENCH, EXISTING OVERHEAD MAINS, NEW UNDERGROUND MAINS, EXISTING UNDERGROUND CABLE, NEW OVERHEAD CONDUCTOR, REMOVE CONDUCTOR, REMOVE EXISTING OVERHEAD, STRING NEW OH CABLE. (2) 'equipment': label contains any of POLE, PILLAR, COLUMN, LANTERN, STREETLIGHT, SLCP, SL FLOOD, SL LANTERN, SL NIGHT WATCH, SHACKLE, JOINT, UGOH, HV ABS, HV USL, LV LINK, SWITCH, FUSE, METER, or label is a short equipment code (UGOH, IE 7.0 IA, IEB5, XXXXX treated as placeholder equipment code) — examples: EXISTING POLE, NEW POLE, REMOVE POLE, REPLACE POLE, EXISTING COLUMN, NEW COLUMN, EXISTING PILLAR, NEW PILLAR, EXISTING LANTERN, NEW LANTERN, REMOVE LANTERN, NEW SL FLOOD, EXISTING SL LANTERN, EXISTING SL NIGHT WATCH, NEW SLCP ZELL, EXISTING SLCP ZELL, REMOVE SL LANTERN, HV ABS (N/C), HV USL (N/C), EXISTING HV ABS CLOSE - REMOVE, NEW HV ABS CLOSE, EXISTING HV USL CLOSED, LV LINK (N/O), JOINT, END SHACKLE, UGOH. (3) 'substation': label contains SUBSTATION or KIOSK. (4) 'boundary': label contains EASEMENT, DEMARCATION, BOUNDARY, or LGA. (5) 'earthing': label is EARTHING or contains EARTH CONDUCTOR or EARTH ELECTRODE. (6) 'annotation': label is a dimension, callout, or arrow descriptor. (7) 'other': anything not matching above — includes TYPE 04 DUCT ARRANGEMENT, FOOTPATH, RMS ROAD, COMPACTED BACKFILL, POLYMERIC CABLE COVER, ROAD LEVEL, DUCT SPACING NTS, XXXXX (if not treating as equipment code). Apply pattern 1 first, then 2, then 3, then 4, then 5, then fall back to 'other'. NEVER leave category null.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Tag to Page Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Tag each entry with the page number from the chunk metadata. If the same legend appears on multiple pages, record it once tagged to page 1. If different pages have different or updated legends, record separate entries per page.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Exhaustiveness Check — Do Not Stop Early
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: After completing your initial extraction pass, count the total number of entries extracted. Then re-scan the legend panel to verify you have not stopped at a section boundary. Many legends are divided into multiple sections (cables, equipment, boundaries, duct arrangements, miscellaneous) separated by horizontal rules or section headings — ensure every section has been processed. DUCT ARRANGEMENT SECTION: Some drawings include a separate duct arrangement cross-section diagram area at the bottom of the legend panel, often showing a schematic cross-section of underground ducts. This area contains its own rows with labels such as DUCT WITH EXISTING CABLE, DUCT WITH NEW CABLE, NEW TRENCH, REMOVE CABLE, REMOVE DUCT & CABLE, REMOVE DUCT, SPARE DUCT, EARTHING, DUCT SPACING NTS — these are regular legend entries and MUST be extracted like any other row. Assign them category 'cable' for duct/cable/trench entries, 'earthing' for EARTHING, and 'other' for annotations like DUCT SPACING NTS. If you see a cross-section diagram in the legend area, always extract every labelled row from it. If the legend contains 10 or more visible rows but you have extracted fewer than 5 entries, you have missed most of the legend and must re-scan. Also verify: (1) every entry has the 'category' field present and is never null; (2) no entry has extra fields beyond the four required (page, symbol_description, label, category) — any other field name is a violation; (3) the 'label' field contains the exact verbatim text from the drawing, not a paraphrase. If any entry fails these checks, correct it before returning the result.

expected_input.description: The processing plan from Step 2 and the actual document files (same as Step 3).

fields.processing_plan: Array of processing entries from Step 2 output
fields.documents_folder: Folder path containing the actual site plan document files

expected_output.description: A structured legend extraction per document: a JSON array of legend entries, each with symbol description, label, category, and page number.

fields.sub-step-extract-legend: JSON array of legend entry objects. Each object must contain EXACTLY these four fields: 'page' (integer), 'symbol_description' (string — visual geometry, not a label paraphrase), 'label' (string — verbatim text from the drawing), 'category' (string — one of: cable, equipment, substation, boundary, earthing, annotation, other — REQUIRED on every entry, never omitted). No additional fields permitted.

actual_input.source: Automatically passed from Step 2 processing_plan; image-based documents processed via vision chunk pipeline

consolidated_output.description: A single consolidated JSON combining all site plan extraction outputs from the document package.

drawing_legend.symbol_description: Description of the visual symbol or line style
drawing_legend.label: Exact text label from the drawing
drawing_legend.category: cable | equipment | substation | boundary | earthing | annotation | other

validation_directives.name: Asset record row completeness
validation_directives.description: Count rows in source spreadsheet vs extracted asset_records. Verify no rows were dropped.

validation_directives.name: Asset field value accuracy
validation_directives.description: Spot-check 5 asset records: verify asset_id, asset_type, and condition_rating match source spreadsheet values exactly.

proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet.summary: Review and extract key asset data from a submitted asset register spreadsheet. The process identifies all relevant spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) in the document package, reads each matched file, and extracts structured asset records covering: Asset ID, Asset Type, Description, Location, Installation Date, Condition Rating, Status, Manufacturer, Model, Serial Number, Capacity/Rating, and Voltage Level. Produces a structured JSON output per document and a consolidated process output.
proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet.search_context: Asset register or asset management spreadsheet files submitted as part of a project document package. Target documents include: Excel spreadsheets (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) containing lists of electrical network assets (transformers, switchgear, cables, poles, substations, meters), asset inventory registers, asset condition assessment spreadsheets, and equipment schedules. Key filename indicators: documents with 'ASSET', 'REGISTER', 'SCHEDULE', 'INVENTORY', 'EQUIPMENT', or 'PLANT' in the filename. Key content indicators: tabular data with columns for asset identifiers, asset types, locations, condition ratings, and technical specifications. Exclude: PDF documents and engineering drawings — these are covered by separate processes.

process_input.description: A folder containing the document package for a project, including all relevant spreadsheet files containing asset register or equipment schedule data.
process_input.format: Folder path containing .xlsx or .xls spreadsheet files

process_output.description: A structured JSON object containing the extracted asset records from the spreadsheet, including all technical specifications and condition data linked to the source file and sheet.

document_categorisation.description: Rules for identifying and categorising a document as an 'Asset Spreadsheet'. When Step 2 assigns a document_category field to each entry in the processing plan, it must apply these rules. A document must satisfy at least two positive indicators and must not satisfy any negative indicators to be categorised as 'Asset Spreadsheet'.

filename_patterns: Contains 'PLANT' in conjunction with register or list
structural_indicators: Contains a header row with column names for asset fields
Contains one row per asset record
Contains columns for asset identification (ID or serial number) and asset type

negative_indicators.description: If any of these are present, the document must NOT be categorised as 'Asset Spreadsheet'.
content_keywords: Method of Supply Requirement (as a section heading — indicates a design brief)
Substation Notes (as a drawing annotation — indicates a site plan)

document_categorisation.decision_rule: If the file has extension .xlsx or .xls and its filename or content contains asset register indicators (ASSET, REGISTER, SCHEDULE, INVENTORY, EQUIPMENT), classify as 'Asset Spreadsheet'. If the file is a PDF, it belongs to another process. When uncertain, prefer 'Asset Spreadsheet' for any tabular spreadsheet file containing rows of asset records.

steps.step_name: Identify and Review Asset Spreadsheet Files
steps.summary: Scan the submitted document package folder, assess each file against the asset spreadsheet search context, and produce a structured list of matched spreadsheet files with extracted metadata. Identifies which files are asset registers, equipment schedules, or inventory spreadsheets.
steps.details: Upload the document package folder path to the Document Reviewer Agent. The agent lists all files in the folder (including .xlsx and .xls), assesses each filename for relevance using an LLM (targeting asset registers, equipment schedules, and inventory spreadsheets), reads file content for matched candidates using openpyxl, and performs a content match analysis. Returns a ranked list of matched documents with metadata. Only documents with overall_confidence > 0.5 are included in the matched documents list. The output file is saved to the OUTPUT directory.

expected_input.description: A folder path containing the document package.

fields.folder_path: Absolute path to the folder containing spreadsheet files (e.g. /documents/ARP4983-84/)
fields.search_context: Asset register or equipment schedule spreadsheet files for a project, including: Excel files (.xlsx, .xls) containing lists of electrical network assets, asset inventory registers, and equipment schedules with columns for Asset ID, Asset Type, Location, Condition Rating, and technical specifications.

expected_output.description: A DocumentReview JSON file listing all matched spreadsheet files with metadata and quality indicators.

fields.documents: Array of matched document objects, each with filename, document_title, document_type, author, date, confidence, content_type, text_quality, and recommended_reader
fields.scan_results: Full scan results for all files including those not matched
fields.total_files_scanned: Integer count of all files scanned
fields.output_file: Path to the saved DocumentReview_*.json file

actual_input.source: User-provided folder path via the frontend folder prompt

steps.summary: Read the Step 1 document review output and the tool registry to determine the best processing tool for each matched spreadsheet file and the extraction method to use.
steps.details: The Document Processing Planner reads the Step 1 DocumentReview output file and the registry.json. For each document in the matched list, it uses an LLM to assess the document's content_type and text_quality and selects the most appropriate processing tool from the registry. Spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls) are text-based and can be read directly via openpyxl. Outputs a ProcessingPlan JSON file with one entry per document.

expected_input.description: The Step 1 DocumentReview output file path, automatically retrieved from the orchestrator state.

fields.step1_output_file: Path to the DocumentReview_*.json file produced by Step 1

expected_output.description: A ProcessingPlan JSON file with a processing entry per matched spreadsheet file.

fields.processing_plan: Array of processing entries, each with: filename, document_type, content_type, text_quality, recommended_reader, processing_tool_id, conversion_required, conversion_type, extraction_method, processing_notes
fields.total_documents: Integer count of entries in processing_plan
fields.output_file: Path to the saved ProcessingPlan_*.json file

actual_input.source: Automatically passed from Step 1 output — no user input required

steps.summary: For each matched asset spreadsheet, extract all asset records including identification, classification, location, technical specifications, condition ratings, and status fields. Each extracted asset record is linked to its source file and sheet.
steps.details: Using the processing plan from Step 2, each matched spreadsheet file is read using the tool specified in processing_tool_id. The extracted tabular content is passed to an LLM which identifies the header row, maps column names to standard asset fields, and extracts each row as a structured asset record. Sub-steps cover: asset identification, asset classification and type, location data, technical specifications (capacity, voltage, ratings), condition and status, and installation/maintenance dates.

sub_steps.details: Extract the unique identifiers for each asset record, including Asset ID, Asset Number, Serial Number, and any network operator assigned reference codes.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Asset ID Columns
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Identify the column(s) in the spreadsheet header row that serve as unique identifiers for each asset. Look for column names such as 'Asset ID', 'Asset Number', 'Asset Ref', 'ID', 'Tag Number', or 'Equipment Number'. Extract the value from each row.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Serial Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the 'Serial Number', 'Serial No', or 'S/N' column. Extract the manufacturer's serial number for each asset record. If no serial number column exists, record as null.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Record Network Operator References
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Identify any network operator specific reference codes (e.g., SAP asset number, MAXIMO work order, or network operator asset tag). These are typically in columns labelled 'SAP ID', 'Network Ref', or 'Operator Ref'.

sub_steps.details: Extract the asset type, category, and classification from each record. This includes the primary asset class (e.g., Transformer, Switchgear, Cable, Pole) and any sub-classification or asset group.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Asset Type Column
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the column(s) labelled 'Asset Type', 'Asset Category', 'Equipment Type', 'Asset Class', or 'Description'. Extract the asset type value for each row.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Asset Description
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Find the 'Description', 'Asset Description', or 'Equipment Description' column. Extract the full descriptive text for each asset record including make, model, and rated specification if combined in the description field.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Note Asset Group or Hierarchy
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If the spreadsheet contains parent-child asset relationships or asset group columns (e.g., 'Parent Asset', 'Asset Group', 'Functional Location'), extract these to preserve the asset hierarchy context.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Superior Functional Location (SFL)
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the column labelled 'Superior Function', 'Superior FL', 'Superior Functional Location', 'Functional Location', or 'FLOC'. This column contains the parent asset reference (e.g. 'POLE01011307' for a streetlight mounted on that pole, or 'CLMN00234567' for a lantern on that column). Extract the raw value exactly as written for each asset record and store it as 'superior_functional_location'. If no such column exists, record as null.

sub_steps.details: Extract all location and site information for each asset record, including physical address, substation reference, feeder reference, or geographic coordinates.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Address and Site
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate columns labelled 'Location', 'Address', 'Site', 'Site Name', or 'Suburb'. Extract the physical location for each asset including street address, suburb, or site identifier.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Network Location References
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Find columns for 'Feeder', 'Feeder ID', 'Substation', 'Zone Substation', 'Pole Reference', 'Span Reference', or 'Circuit'. These define where the asset sits within the electrical network topology.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Record GPS or Grid Coordinates
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If geographic coordinate columns exist (e.g., 'Latitude', 'Longitude', 'Easting', 'Northing', 'MGA', 'GDA'), extract the coordinate values for each asset.

sub_steps.details: Extract the technical rating and specification data for each asset including capacity, voltage level, manufacturer, model, and any rated parameters.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Capacity and Ratings
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate columns for 'Capacity', 'Rating', 'kVA', 'MVA', 'Amps', 'Current Rating', or 'Rated Capacity'. Extract the rated capacity with units for each asset. For transformers, capture both primary and secondary voltage ratings if available.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Voltage Level
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Find the 'Voltage Level', 'Voltage', 'kV', 'HV Voltage', or 'LV Voltage' column. Extract the operating voltage level for each asset expressed in kV or V.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Manufacturer and Model
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate 'Manufacturer', 'Make', 'Brand', 'Model', 'Model Number', or 'Type Number' columns. Extract the manufacturer name and model designation for each asset.

sub_steps.details: Extract the current condition rating, asset status (active, retired, proposed), and any maintenance or inspection flags for each asset record.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Condition Rating
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the 'Condition', 'Condition Rating', 'Condition Score', or 'Asset Health' column. Extract the condition rating for each asset. Condition is often expressed as a numeric score (1–5), a text grade (Good, Fair, Poor), or a percentage health index.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Asset Status
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Find the 'Status', 'Asset Status', 'Operational Status', or 'Active/Retired' column. Extract the current operational status for each asset (e.g., Active, In-Service, Retired, Proposed, Decommissioned).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Inspection and Maintenance Dates
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate date columns such as 'Installation Date', 'Commissioned Date', 'Last Inspection', 'Next Inspection', 'Last Maintenance', or 'Replacement Due'. Extract these dates for each asset record.

expected_input.description: The ProcessingPlan from Step 2 indicating which tool to use per document, and the spreadsheet files themselves.

fields.processing_plan: Array of processing entries from Step 2 output
fields.documents_folder: Folder path containing the actual spreadsheet files

expected_output.description: Structured asset records extracted from the spreadsheet, with each record containing all available identification, classification, location, technical, and condition fields.

fields.asset_records: Array of asset record objects — each with: asset_id, serial_number, asset_type, description, location, address, feeder, voltage_level, capacity_rating, manufacturer, model, condition_rating, status, installation_date, last_inspection_date, superior_functional_location, source_sheet, source_row
fields.total_assets: Integer count of extracted asset records
fields.sheets_processed: Array of sheet names that were read from the spreadsheet
fields.source_documents: Array of spreadsheet filenames that contributed to the extracted asset records

actual_input.source: Automatically passed from Step 2 processing_plan; documents read via tool-extract-spreadsheet-content

steps.summary: Perform a structured audit of the POLE tab to verify data presence, clean Functional Location identifiers, and validate mandatory equipment characteristics.
steps.details: This step executes specific domain logic for Pole assets. It includes data cleaning for ID matching, format verification for substation hierarchy, and mandatory field completion checks for assembly items.

sub_steps.details: Verify the presence of the POLE tab and confirm it contains data before proceeding.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Worksheet Presence & Data Scan
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the tab labeled 'POLE'. If the tab is missing or contains no data records, mark as 'No Data' and terminate the POLE validation process. If data is present, proceed.

sub_steps.details: Extract and clean identifiers for cross-referencing with Design Drawings.

instructional_sub_steps.title: FLOC Cleaning Rule
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Extract Functional Location Numbers. Remove all leading letters and zeros for comparison (e.g., convert 'POLE00165863' to '165863').

instructional_sub_steps.title: Substation Superior Function Validation
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: For entries identified as 'Pole Substations', verify the 'Superior Function' field matches the mask 'POLEXXXXXXXXX'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Schedule Consistency Check
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Extract asset numbers from the 'POLE SCHEDULE TABLE' and verify consistency against the TAL and design drawings.

sub_steps.details: Check for mandatory data entry and hierarchical integrity between assembly records.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Mandatory Field Audit
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Ensure the following columns are populated: Special Use Code, EQ Reference Equipment Desc, EQ Technical Object Type, FLOC Technical Object, Location, and Equipment Owner.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Assembly & Hierarchy Validation
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: For every Assembly Number, verify: 1. Superior Functional Location matches the top-tab Functional Location. 2. FLOC Construction Type matches the design environment (Urban, Rural, Semi-Rural, Coastal). 3. Presence of Equipment Number and Technical Object attributes.

expected_input.description: The extracted data from the POLE worksheet and the associated Design Drawings/TAL for cross-referencing.

expected_output.description: A validation report for Pole assets highlighting missing fields, ID mismatches, and hierarchy errors.

fields.validation_errors: List of missing mandatory fields or hierarchy mismatches

actual_input.source: Output from Step 3 filtered for POLE tab

consolidated_output.description: A single consolidated JSON combining all asset records extracted from all matched spreadsheet files in the document package.

document_review.search_context:
Scan the project document folder for all relevant documents. Use the search context and document categorisation rules from the process knowledge files (proc-extract-design-brief-info, proc-extract-site-plan-info, and proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet) to determine which files to include and how to classify each document type. For each file found, read the document content and classify it as one of: 'Design Brief', 'Architectural Brief', 'Services Brief', 'Town Planning Report', 'Reticulation Drawing', 'Site Plan Drawing', 'Substation Diagram', 'Network Diagram', or 'TAL'. Any Excel spreadsheet file (.xlsx or .xls) containing asset register, equipment schedule, or inventory data (columns for Asset ID, Asset Type, Location, Condition Rating, or similar) must be classified as 'TAL' (Technical Asset List). Classification must be based on document content — what the document actually contains — not its filename. Produce a structured document list with metadata for all matched files.

=== Knowledge: Extract Information from Design Brief ===
Search Context: Design brief documents for a customer connections application submitted to Endeavour Energy. Target documents include: project design briefs (PDF documents containing a 'Method of Supply Requirement' section and a 'Determination of Funding Requirements' section), customer connection offer documents, and any associated architectural, services, or town planning reports. Key filename indicators: documents with 'DESIGN_BRIEF', 'BRIEF', or 'ARP' (Endeavour Energy project reference) in the filename. Key content indicators: structured sections covering HV Works, LV Works, SL Works, Earthing, Easements, Substation works, and a funding determination table listing items with responsible party and funding category. Exclude: reticulation drawings and engineering drawing files identified by 'RETIC' or 'DWG' in the filename — these are Site Plan documents and must be assigned document_category 'Site Plan'.
Document Category 'Design Brief': Positive filename patterns: ["Contains 'BRIEF' or 'brief' (e.g. DESIGN_BRIEF, Design_Brief)", "Contains 'DB' as a standalone component of the filename", "Contains 'ARP' followed by a project number (typical Endeavour Energy design brief naming convention)", "Contains 'DESIGN' in conjunction with project or ARP references", "Contains 'architectural', 'services', 'structural', or 'town_planning'"]. Document type values: ['Design Brief', 'Architectural Brief', 'Services Brief', 'Structural Brief', 'Town Planning Report', 'Connection Offer', 'Customer Connection Brief']. Negative filename patterns (exclude from this category): ["Contains 'RETIC' or 'retic' (indicates a reticulation drawing)", "Contains 'DWG' (indicates an engineering drawing file)", "Contains 'SITEPLAN' or 'site_plan'", "Contains 'SUBSTATION' or 'substation' as the primary descriptor (not as a section heading within a brief)"].

=== Knowledge: Extract Information from Site Plans ===
Search Context: Site plan drawings for an electrical network project submitted to Endeavour Energy. Target documents include: electrical network diagrams showing HV and LV infrastructure, substation layout drawings (pole substations, padmount substations, kiosk substations), site layout plans with electrical asset annotations, and engineering drawings containing a 'Substation Notes' or 'Sub Notes' callout table. Key filename indicators: documents with 'RETIC' (reticulation drawing), 'DWG' (engineering drawing), or a drawing number prefix (e.g. D followed by digits, A followed by digits) in the filename. Key content indicators: a Substation Notes annotation block, a NOTES section with numbered notes, ATTENTION/WARNING/CAUTION callouts, easement creation notes, and field check compliance notes. Engineering drawings produced to Endeavour Energy or similar network operator standards. Target file types: PDF engineering drawings, scanned drawing sets. Exclude: design brief documents identified by 'BRIEF' or 'DESIGN_BRIEF' in the filename.
Document Category 'Site Plan': Positive filename patterns: ["Contains 'RETIC' or 'retic' (e.g. ARP4983_RETIC, D526458_ARP4983_RETIC — indicates a reticulation drawing)", "Contains 'DWG' as part of the filename (indicates an engineering drawing)", "Contains 'SITEPLAN', 'SITE_PLAN', or 'site_plan'", "Contains 'SUBSTATION' or 'SUB' as the primary descriptor (not as a section heading within a brief)", 'Drawing number prefix format (e.g. D followed by digits, A followed by digits) typical of network operator drawing numbering conventions', "Contains 'ELEC', 'ELECTRICAL', or 'NETWORK' in conjunction with a drawing reference"]. Document type values: ['Reticulation Drawing', 'Electrical Network Diagram', 'Site Plan Drawing', 'Substation Drawing', 'Network Diagram', 'Engineering Drawing', 'HV Network Diagram', 'LV Network Diagram', 'Pole Substation Drawing', 'Padmount Substation Drawing']. Negative filename patterns (exclude from this category): ["Contains 'BRIEF' or 'brief' (e.g. DESIGN_BRIEF, Design_Brief)", "Contains 'ARP' as a standalone prefix without a drawing number suffix"].

=== Knowledge: Extract Information from Asset Spreadsheet ===
Search Context: Asset register or asset management spreadsheet files submitted as part of a project document package. Target documents include: Excel spreadsheets (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) containing lists of electrical network assets (transformers, switchgear, cables, poles, substations, meters), asset inventory registers, asset condition assessment spreadsheets, and equipment schedules. Key filename indicators: documents with 'ASSET', 'REGISTER', 'SCHEDULE', 'INVENTORY', 'EQUIPMENT', or 'PLANT' in the filename. Key content indicators: tabular data with columns for asset identifiers, asset types, locations, condition ratings, and technical specifications. Exclude: PDF documents and engineering drawings — these are covered by separate processes.
Document Category 'TAL': Positive filename patterns: ["Contains 'ASSET' or 'asset' (e.g. ASSET_REGISTER, Asset_List)", "Contains 'REGISTER' or 'register'", "Contains 'SCHEDULE' or 'schedule' in the filename", "Contains 'INVENTORY' or 'inventory'", "Contains 'EQUIPMENT' or 'equipment'", "Contains 'PLANT' in conjunction with register or list", 'Has file extension .xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, or .xlsb']. Document type values: ['Asset Register', 'Asset Schedule', 'Equipment Schedule', 'Plant Register', 'Asset Inventory', 'Asset Condition Assessment', 'Network Asset Register']. Negative filename patterns (exclude from this category): ["Contains 'RETIC' or 'retic' (indicates a reticulation drawing)", "Contains 'DWG' (indicates an engineering drawing file)", "Contains 'BRIEF' or 'brief' (indicates a design brief)"].

Substation Clearances and Standards.description: Relocate existing pole 283061, 283062 & 283063 to the new road alignment as per MDI0031 requirements.

EarthingTechnicalRequirements.description: Install new Air break switch earthing as per EDI0006 & drawing B348252

SpecificEarthingStandards.description: All OH line design as per MDI0031 & MCI0005

LV Works.description: Remove existing OH LV conductor from pole 283062 to pole 426121.
Install new OH LV conductor 7/4.50AAC from new pole 2 to pole 426121.

Redirect the existing LV OH conductor to new poles 1 & 2. If the existing LV conductor length is insufficient, terminate existing LV conductor 7/4.50AAV on new pole 1 & install new span of LV bare 7/4.50AAC from new poles 1 to 2.

Compliance Standards.description: All OH line design as per MDI0031 & MCI0005.

LandInterestGuidelines.description: No specific mention of 'Land Interests' section or checkbox for 'NA' in the provided sections.

RequiredEasements.description: No explicit details regarding required easements for new HV/LV lines or substation sites found in the provided sections.

PropertyAccessConditions.description: No specific conditions regarding access to private property or creation of right-of-ways for Endeavour Energy assets found in the provided sections.

Primary HV Method of Supply.description: Relocate existing pole 283061, 283062 & 283063 to the new road alignment as per MDI0031 requirements.
Remove existing OH HV conductor from poles 283061 to 283063.
Remove existing OH HV conductor from pole 283062 to pole 426121.
Install new ABS on new pole 3. New Air break switch earthing as per EDI0006 & drawing B348252.
Install new OH HV conductor 19/3.25 AAC Neptune from new poles 1 to 3.
Install new OH HV conductor 7/4.50 AAC from new pole 2 to pole 426121.

Compliance Standards.description: All OH line design as per MDI0031 & MCI0005.

Mandatory Transaction References.description: Unique alphanumeric reference code ARP4983A2 must be quoted in the EFT transaction field.

Remittance and Restriction Requirements.description: Email for remittance copies: remittances@endeavourenergy.com.au. Credit cards and cheques are not accepted.

Invoice Policy.description: A formal Tax Invoice will be provided following the receipt of payment.

SL_Works.description: Relocate existing pole 283061, 283062 & 283063 to the new road alignment as per MDI0031 requirements.
Remove existing OH SL conductor from pole 283062 to pole 426121 and from pole 283062 to pole 283063.
Remove existing SLCP 704 on pole 283062.
Existing OH SL conductor to be redirected from pole 426129 to new pole 1 & pole 2.
Install new SL conductor 25mm^2 2C from new pole 2 to pole 426121 and from new pole 2 to pole 3.
Remove all existing street lighting on poles 283061 & 283063.
Install new streetlighting on new poles 1 & 3 as per LDI0001 requirements.
Install new additional luminaires and light pole as per the lighting assessment.
